//: Standalone example compile: plain tsc emits src -> dist (no bundler), and `node` runs dist/main.js directly. `module: ESNext` keeps the relative `./contracts.js` import specifier verbatim in the emit so node ESM resolves it at runtime, while `moduleResolution: Bundler` lets tsc map that `.js` specifier back to its `.ts` source at compile time. `types: [node]` pulls process/console without dragging @types/bun's globals in. The @rhombus-std/config.* bare imports resolve to each package's source types here (tsc's `types` export condition), which is where the providers' `declare module` augmentations enter this program so addJsonFile/addEnvironmentVariables/addCommandLine typecheck.
